hero_title: Manage Sanctions and Adverse Media Screenings
hero_subtitle: SFTY connects sanctions and adverse media screening results into structured case workflows that support review, evidence preservation, and regulatory readiness for digital asset businesses.

trust_card_2_value: Corporate verification, UBO review and entity risk

problem_title: Screening results remain siloed and disconnected
problem_description: Compliance teams struggle to unify sanctions, PEP and adverse media alerts when outputs from multiple screening providers are scattered across platforms. This creates gaps in case management, slows alert triage and weakens the audit trail needed for effective review and reporting.
problem_point_1_value: KYC, KYB, KYT and Travel Rule outputs sit in separate tools
problem_point_2_value: Teams move data between dashboards, spreadsheets and internal notes
problem_point_3_value: Regulatory changes are hard to map into internal workflows
problem_point_4_value: Evidence and decision history are difficult to reconstruct later
solution_description: SFTY integrates sanctions, PEP and adverse media outputs from multiple providers into a single case management workflow to streamline alert triage, preserve audit evidence and support compliance operations.
workflow_title: From screening alerts to case decisions
workflow_description: SFTY collects sanctions and adverse media alerts from multiple providers, routes them for review, opens cases, records decisions and preserves evidence for reporting.
workflow_step_1_description: KYC, KYB, KYT and screening outputs enter SFTY
workflow_step_2_description: Risk rules route checks into the right workflow
workflow_step_3_description: A case opens with customer, wallet and provider context
workflow_step_4_description: Teams review, decide and record the outcome
workflow_step_5_description: Logs, decisions and reports stay ready for audit

why_sfty_card_3_description: Preserve evidence, reviewer actions and decision history
why_sfty_card_4_description: Give compliance, risk and leadership one operating view

ask_sfty_helper_text: AI assistant for product, deployment and compliance questions
faq_description: Clear answers on provider orchestration, Travel Rule workflows, audit evidence and enterprise compliance operations.
faq_1_question: What does SFTY do for sanctions screening?
faq_1_answer: SFTY connects sanctions and adverse media screening outputs into structured cases, decisions and workflows to help compliance teams manage risk across wallets, customers and transactions in one platform.
faq_2_question: How does SFTY manage screening case workflows?
faq_2_answer: SFTY orchestrates multiple screening providers’ results, routes alerts for review, opens cases with linked evidence and applies internal rules to support consistent sanction-related compliance workflows.
faq_3_question: How does SFTY preserve audit-ready evidence?
faq_3_answer: SFTY preserves decision logs, case histories and all underlying screening evidence in an organized, audit-ready format to support regulatory review and internal inspections.
faq_4_question: Can SFTY integrate with existing compliance stacks?
faq_4_answer: Yes. SFTY sits above existing KYC, KYB, KYT, Travel Rule and screening providers and integrates their outputs without requiring replacement or disruption of current systems.
faq_5_question: Does SFTY replace sanctions or screening providers?
faq_5_answer: No. SFTY does not replace underlying sanctions or screening providers but aggregates and structures their outputs into operational compliance workflows and evidence packages.
final_title: Connect screening outputs into structured case workflows
final_description: Review how SFTY links sanctions and adverse media results into audit-ready cases, decisions and regulatory evidence packages.
